docs.title: Video Annotation Tool
docs.text: The Video Annotation Tool is a PyQt6 desktop application for loading, editing, and exporting OSL-style sports-video datasets.

docs.title: What You Can Do
docs.text:
• Create, open, close, save, and export dataset JSON projects.
  • Manage samples and multi-input clips from the Dataset Explorer.
  • Annotate across five modes:
  • Classification (labels)
  • Localization (events)
  • Description (captions)
  • Dense Description (dense_captions)
  • Question/Answer (questions + answers)
  • Use global undo/redo for tracked edits.
  • Download from and upload to Hugging Face from the Data menu.

docs.title: Quick Links
docs.text:

  • Getting Started

docs.title: OSL JSON Format (As Used By This App)
docs.text: This page describes the OSL-style structure expected and produced by the current Video Annotation Tool.

docs.title: Top-Level Structure
docs.text:
Required/standard fields:

  • version (string)
  • date (string)
  • dataset_name (string)
  • description (string)
  • modalities (array, usually ["video"])
  • metadata (object)
  • labels (object)
  • questions (array)
  • data (array)

Unknown root keys are preserved.

docs.title: Labels Schema (labels)
docs.text:
Each head is a key under labels:

"labels": {
  "action": {
    "type": "single_label",
    "labels": ["pass", "shot"]
  },
  "attributes": {
    "type": "multi_label",
    "labels": ["left_foot", "header"]
  }
}

docs.title: Sample Structure (data[])
docs.text:
Each sample typically contains:

  • id (string)
  • inputs (array of input objects, each usually has type + path)
  • Optional task blocks:
  • labels (classification)
  • events (localization)
  • captions (description)
  • dense_captions (dense description)
  • answers (Q/A)
  • Optional metadata
  • Any additional custom keys are preserved.

docs.title: inputs
docs.text:
Example:

"inputs": [
  {"type": "video", "path": "test/action_0/clip_0.mp4", "fps": 25.0}
]

Multi-view samples can include multiple input entries.

docs.title: Classification payload (labels per sample)
docs.text:
• single-label head: {"label": "shot"}
  • multi-label head: {"labels": ["header", "left_foot"]}
  • smart predictions may include confidence_score

docs.title: Localization payload (events)
docs.text:
"events": [
  {"head": "action", "label": "pass", "position_ms": 1234}
]

Smart localization events may include confidence_score.

docs.title: Description payload (captions)
docs.text:
"captions": [
  {"lang": "en", "text": "A short caption."}
]

docs.title: Dense payload (dense_captions)
docs.text:
The current dense editor uses point timestamps:

"dense_captions": [
  {"position_ms": 4567, "lang": "en", "text": "Dense description."}
]

docs.title: Q/A payload (questions + answers)
docs.text:
Top-level bank:

"questions": [
  {"id": "q1", "question": "How are you?"}
]

Per-sample sparse answers:

"answers": [
  {"question_id": "q1", "answer": "I am fine."}
]

docs.title: Save-Time Behavior
docs.text:
On save/export, the app:

  • ensures unique sample IDs
  • normalizes/filters invalid or empty answer entries
  • removes empty optional task blocks
  • rewrites input paths relative to the output JSON location
  • preserves unknown root/sample fields

docs.title: About
docs.text:
The Video Annotation Tool is developed by OpenSportsLab for sports-video dataset curation and annotation.

  • Project Lead: Silvio Giancola
  • Front-End Developer: Jintao Ma / Silvio Giancola
  • Repository: https://github.com/OpenSportsLab/VideoAnnotationTool
  • License: Dual-licensed (AGPL-3.0 / Commercial)

docs.title: Classification
docs.text:
1. Select a sample in the Dataset Explorer.
  2. Open CLS.
  3. Choose labels in each head.
  4. Changes persist immediately when they are effective.

docs.title: Localization
docs.text:
1. Select a sample and open LOC.
  2. Use spotting buttons to add events at current time.
  3. Edit or delete events from the event table.
  4. Optional: run smart inference for a selected head.

docs.title: Description
docs.text:
1. Select a sample and open DESC.
  2. Edit caption text.
  3. Autosave stores the caption in captions.

docs.title: Dense Description
docs.text:
1. Select a sample and open DENSE.
  2. Click Add New Description.
  3. Enter text in the modal; event is stored at current position_ms.
  4. Edit time/text from the table when needed.

docs.title: Question/Answer
docs.text:
1. Open Q/A.
  2. Add or select a question tab.
  3. Enter the sample answer.
  4. Answers are stored as sparse answers keyed by question_id.

docs.title: Batch Tools
docs.text: The app supports Hugging Face dataset transfer from the Data menu and script/API workflows for batch conversion.

docs.title: Download Dataset from HF...
docs.text:
• Opens a dialog for:
  • HF URL
  • output directory
  • optional token
  • dry-run mode
  • Supports:
  • JSON file URLs (.../blob/.../annotations.json)
  • Parquet folder URLs (.../tree/.../<folder>)
  • On successful non-dry-run JSON download, source metadata is written into the JSON root:
  • hf_source_url
  • hf_repo_id
  • hf_branch

docs.title: Upload Dataset to HF...
docs.text:
Requires an opened dataset JSON from disk.

Upload modes:

  • Upload as JSON: uploads current dataset JSON plus files referenced by data[].inputs[].path in one commit.
  • Parquet + WebDataset: converts locally, then uploads generated Parquet/shards (samples-per-shard configurable).

If repository/branch is missing, the app can prompt to create it and retry.

docs.title: Download referenced files
docs.text:
python test_data/download_osl_hf.py \
  --url <HF_JSON_OR_FOLDER_URL> \
  --output-dir <LOCAL_DIR> \
  --types video \
  --dry-run

docs.title: Upload referenced files
docs.text:
python test_data/upload_osl_hf.py \
  --repo-id <org/repo> \
  --json-path <local_dataset.json> \
  --revision main

docs.title: Python Conversion API
docs.text:
from annotation_tool.tools import convert_json_to_parquet, convert_parquet_to_json

convert_json_to_parquet(json_path="annotations.json", media_root=".", output_dir="out_parquet")
convert_parquet_to_json(dataset_dir="out_parquet", output_json_path="reconstructed.json")

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text:
Contributions are welcome.

Start with the repository contribution guide:

  • https://github.com/OpenSportsLab/VideoAnnotationTool/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

Typical flow:

  1. Fork the repository.
  2. Create a feature branch.
  3. Add tests/docs for your change.
  4. Open a pull request.

docs.title: Edit Sample IDs
docs.text:
• Double-click a top-level sample row in the Dataset Explorer tree to rename its id.
  • IDs are kept unique (__2, __3, ...) when collisions occur.

docs.title: Edit Header Fields
docs.text:
In the Dataset Explorer header table:

  • Editable known fields: version, date, dataset_name, description, metadata
  • Unknown/custom root keys are shown read-only
  • Raw JSON tab reflects current in-memory state

docs.title: Edit Annotations
docs.text:
• Classification: change selected labels in head tabs.
  • Localization: edit event time/head/label directly in the table.
  • Description: edit caption text.
  • Dense: edit event text/time in the table.
  • Q/A: edit answers per selected question.

docs.title: Remove Data
docs.text:
• Right-click a sample row: Remove Sample
  • Right-click an input child row: Remove Input

If a sample loses its last input, the whole sample is removed.

docs.title: Which file types can I add as inputs?
docs.text:
The Add Data picker supports common video/image extensions (.mp4, .avi, .mov, .mkv, .jpg, .jpeg, .png, .bmp).

Video preview/playback is video-only.

docs.title: Why did a sample ID change to __2?
docs.text: When duplicate or missing IDs are loaded, the app normalizes them to unique IDs (for example clip_dup, clip_dup__2).

docs.title: Where are localization label colors stored?
docs.text: In application settings (QSettings), not in the dataset JSON.

docs.title: Why is HF upload disabled?
docs.text: Upload is available only when a dataset JSON is currently opened from disk (not unsaved in-memory only).

docs.title: Why does a recent dataset entry disappear?
docs.text: If the file no longer exists, the app removes the stale entry from recents.

docs.title: Where do I report bugs?
docs.text:
Open an issue at:

  • https://github.com/OpenSportsLab/VideoAnnotationTool/issues

docs.title: 1. Launch
docs.text:
Start the app from the repository root:

python annotation_tool/main.py

You will land on the Welcome screen.

docs.title: 2. Create Or Open A Dataset
docs.text:
• Create New Dataset for a blank OSL dataset.
  • Load Dataset to open an existing JSON.
  • You can also reopen files from the recent-datasets list.

docs.title: 3. Add Samples
docs.text:
In the Dataset Explorer:

  • Click Add Data.
  • Select files or folders.
  • Selected folders are treated as multi-input samples (for multi-view workflows).

docs.title: 4. Annotate
docs.text:
Use the right-side tabs:

  • CLS for classification labels
  • LOC for timestamped events
  • DESC for clip-level captions
  • DENSE for timestamped dense captions
  • Q/A for question bank + per-sample answers

docs.title: 5. Save
docs.text:
• Ctrl+S saves to the current JSON path.
  • Ctrl+Shift+S saves as a new JSON file.

When you close with unsaved changes, you can Save, Save As, Close Without Saving, or Cancel.

docs.title: GUI Overview
docs.text: The workspace has three regions: Dataset Explorer (left), Media Center (middle), and Annotation Editor tabs (right).

docs.title: Welcome Screen
docs.text:
• Create New Dataset
  • Load Dataset
  • Recent datasets list
  • Links to docs/tutorial and GitHub

docs.title: Left: Dataset Explorer
docs.text:
• Tree of samples (parent row) and inputs (child rows)
  • Filter: Show All, Show Labelled, Show Smart Labelled, Show Not Labelled
  • Add Data, Prev, Next, Clear All
  • Context menu on tree rows for removing a sample or a single input
  • Header inspector tabs:
  • Known header fields (editable)
  • Unknown/custom root keys (read-only)
  • Raw JSON preview

docs.title: Middle: Media Center
docs.text:
• Video preview
  • Timeline + zoom
  • Marker overlays (mode-dependent)
  • Playback controls (seek/playback rate)
  • Mute icon button (state persists via app settings)

docs.title: Classification (CLS)
docs.text:
• Edit label heads and labels
  • Supports single-label and multi-label heads
  • Manual edits are saved immediately on effective change
  • Smart inference per head with confirm/reject

docs.title: Localization (LOC)
docs.text:
• Spot events at current playhead time
  • Head/label add/rename/delete + per-label colors
  • Event table supports edit, delete, confirm/reject smart events
  • Smart inference with model + time-range prompts

docs.title: Description (DESC)
docs.text:
• Clip-level text editor for captions
  • Autosaves after short idle delay when text changes

docs.title: Dense Description (DENSE)
docs.text:
• Add New Description opens a modal and inserts at current playhead time
  • Dense events are editable/deletable in the table
  • Events are kept chronological by position_ms

docs.title: Question/Answer (Q/A)
docs.text:
• Dataset-level question tabs (q1, q2, ...)
  • + tab adds a question
  • Right-click question tab to rename/delete
  • Per-sample answer editor autosaves after short idle delay

docs.title: Option 1: Use Pre-Built Binaries
docs.text:
Pre-built binaries for macOS, Windows, and Linux are available on GitHub Releases:

  • https://github.com/OpenSportsLab/VideoAnnotationTool/releases

Download the asset for your platform and run it directly.

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.12 recommended
  • A working Qt multimedia backend (installed with PyQt6)
git clone https://github.com/OpenSportsLab/VideoAnnotationTool.git
cd VideoAnnotationTool

Create and activate an environment:

conda create -n VideoAnnotationTool python=3.12 -y
conda activate VideoAnnotationTool

Install dependencies:

pip install -r requirements.txt

Run the app:

python annotation_tool/main.py

docs.title: Optional Setup
docs.text:
• Hugging Face access for gated datasets:
huggingface-cli login
  • If some videos fail to decode, install FFmpeg and transcode unsupported files to H.264 MP4 (see Troubleshooting).

docs.title: Loading
docs.text:
Use File > Load Dataset (Ctrl+O) to open an OSL JSON file.

The app normalizes loaded content (for example, missing IDs) while preserving unknown root/sample fields when possible.

docs.title: Saving
docs.text:
• Save Dataset (Ctrl+S): writes to current path.
  • Save Dataset As (Ctrl+Shift+S): writes to a new path.

On write, paths in data[].inputs[].path are rewritten relative to the chosen save location.

docs.title: Close With Unsaved Changes
docs.text:
If the dataset is dirty and you close/quit, the app prompts:

  • Save
  • Save As
  • Close Without Saving
  • Cancel

docs.title: What Is Persisted
docs.text:
• Core OSL fields (labels, events, captions, dense_captions, questions, answers)
  • Unknown/custom root/sample fields

docs.title: Not persisted in dataset JSON
docs.text: • Localization label_colors (stored in app settings)

docs.title: Project
docs.text:
• Ctrl+O: Load dataset JSON
  • Ctrl+S: Save dataset
  • Ctrl+Shift+S: Save dataset as
  • Ctrl+D: Open HF download dialog
  • Ctrl+U: Open HF upload dialog
  • Ctrl+E: Reserved (settings placeholder)
  • Ctrl/Cmd+Q: Quit

docs.title: Undo/Redo
docs.text:
• Ctrl/Cmd+Z: Undo
  • Ctrl/Cmd+Shift+Z or platform redo key: Redo

docs.title: Media
docs.text:
• Space: Play/Pause
  • Left: Seek backward ~40 ms
  • Right: Seek forward ~40 ms
  • Ctrl+Left: Seek backward 1 second
  • Ctrl+Right: Seek forward 1 second
  • Ctrl+Shift+Left: Seek backward 5 seconds
  • Ctrl+Shift+Right: Seek forward 5 seconds

docs.title: Notes
docs.text: • S currently shows an info hint in localization workflows; it does not directly set event time.

docs.title: App Fails To Start
docs.text:
• Confirm your environment is active.
  • Reinstall dependencies:
pip install -r requirements.txt

docs.title: Hugging Face Transfer Errors
docs.text:
• Ensure huggingface_hub is installed.
  • Authenticate if dataset access is gated:
huggingface-cli login
  • For upload failures:
  • Repository Not Found: create repo or let the app create it from the prompt.
  • Revision/Branch Not Found: create branch or let the app create it from the prompt.

docs.title: Download URL 404 / Not Found
docs.text:
• Verify the URL points to the intended file or folder in the dataset repo.
  • If a previously successful URL is now invalid, reselect/correct it in the dialog.

docs.title: Video Playback Error Or Black Screen
docs.text:
Some codecs are not decoded by your platform backend. Convert to H.264/AAC MP4:

ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -vcodec libx264 -acodec aac output.mp4

docs.title: No Playback After Selecting A Row
docs.text: If the selected input is non-video (for example text metadata), this is expected: the row stays selectable but media playback is not started.
